commit.message:
Fix/new user donation link (#94)

* fix: auto-create DB user for new Clerk users hitting protected routes

When a new user completes Clerk SSO (e.g., via a donation link) and
lands on a protected route, the DB user record didn't exist yet because
it was only created on /profile or invitation acceptance. This caused
'User not found' errors.

Add ensureUserExists() utility that finds or creates the DB user record
using the Clerk session, and use it in checkOrganizationAccess() and
withOrgAuth() instead of bare findUnique lookups.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: deduplicate user find-or-create logic

Replace inline find-or-create code in profile/page.tsx and
invite/[token]/page.tsx with the shared ensureUserExists() utility.
Simplify its return type to always return a full User record.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update park-babelsberg/datenschutz.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve P/Invoke documentation: type warnings, sample fix, troubleshooting (#51987)

* Add warnings about C long and wchar_t cross-platform type differences

The type marshalling table maps C# long to int64_t but does not warn that
C long is 32-bit on Windows and 64-bit on 64-bit Unix. Add an Important
note cross-referencing the CLong guidance in best-practices.md.

Also add a note about wchar_t being UTF-32 on non-Windows platforms,
which affects string marshalling decisions for cross-platform P/Invoke.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ftw samples to use Utf8 instead of Utf16

The C ftw() function takes char* parameters (UTF-8 on Linux/macOS),
not wchar_t*. Using StringMarshalling.Utf16 is incorrect and would
cause the strings to be marshalled in the wrong encoding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add guidance on when calling conventions can be omitted

Calling conventions only need to be specified on Windows x86, where
Stdcall and Cdecl differ. On x64, ARM, and ARM64 there is only one
calling convention, making the attribute unnecessary. Add explicit
DO/DON'T recommendations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add troubleshooting table, GC.KeepAlive guidance, and header trust note

Add three new sections to the native interop best practices:

1. Troubleshooting P/Invoke failures: a symptom-to-cause-to-fix table
   covering DllNotFoundException, EntryPointNotFoundException,
   AccessViolationException, silent corruption, intermittent crashes,
   and heap corruption.

2. Preventing delegate collection with GC.KeepAlive: explains that
   GetFunctionPointerForDelegate does not root the source delegate,
   with a code example showing proper GC.KeepAlive usage.

3. Resolving conflicts between documentation and native headers:
   advises developers to treat native headers as the authoritative
   source for P/Invoke signatures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Adding agent plugin and documentatoin (#333)

Closes #269 

## Description

ships a Copilot plugin for the repo itself.


### Copilot Plugin (`.github/plugin/`)

Ships a repo-level Copilot plugin with an agent file and the same 7
skills:

```
.github/plugin/
├── plugin.json
├── agents/
│   └── winapp-cli/
│       └── winapp.agent.md      # system prompt
└── skills/
    └── winapp-cli/
        ├── setup/SKILL.md
        ├── package/SKILL.md
        ├── identity/SKILL.md
        ├── signing/SKILL.md
        ├── manifest/SKILL.md
        ├── troubleshoot/SKILL.md
        └── frameworks/SKILL.md
```

The agent file (`winapp.agent.md`) includes:
- Full command reference with syntax + descriptions
- Decision tree for common workflows
- Framework-specific packaging guidance
- Error diagnosis patterns
- Critical rules (e.g. "Run `winapp --cli-schema` for the full CLI
reference")

### Simplified AI Documentation

Removed redundant docs in favor of the skill-based approach:
- **Deleted** `docs/llm-context.md` (full CLI reference doc — now
covered by skills + `winapp --cli-schema`)
- **Deleted** `docs/using-with-llms.md` (guide — now covered by README
section + skills)
- **Merged** two README AI sections into a single concise "🤖 Using with
AI Coding Agents" section
- **Updated** `llms.txt` with Copilot plugin structure

commit.message:
Bring Anthropic usechat backend to OpenAI-level tool-use parity and stream robustness (#2971)

This updates `pkg/aiusechat/anthropic` from partial implementation to
full backend parity for core tool-use orchestration and stream behavior.
The main gaps were unimplemented tool lifecycle methods, missing
persisted tool-use UI state, and weaker disconnect/error handling versus
the OpenAI backend.

- **Tool-use lifecycle parity (critical path)**
  - Implemented Anthropic backend support for:
    - `UpdateToolUseData`
    - `RemoveToolUseCall`
    - `GetFunctionCallInputByToolCallId`
- Wired `pkg/aiusechat/usechat-backend.go` to call Anthropic
implementations instead of stubs.
- Added Anthropic run-step nil-message guard so `nil` responses are not
wrapped into `[]GenAIMessage{nil}`.

- **Persisted tool-use state in Anthropic native messages**
  - Added internal `ToolUseData` storage on Anthropic `tool_use` blocks.
- Ensured internal-only fields are stripped before API requests via
`Clean()`.

- **UI conversion parity for reloaded history**
- Extended `ConvertToUIMessage()` to emit `data-tooluse` parts when
tool-use metadata exists, in addition to `tool-{name}` parts.

- **Streaming UX parity for tool argument deltas**
  - Added `aiutil.SendToolProgress(...)` calls during:
    - `input_json_delta` (incremental updates)
    - `content_block_stop` for `tool_use` (final update)

- **Disconnect/stream robustness**
  - Added `sse.Err()` checks in event handling and decode-error path.
- Added partial-text extraction on client disconnect and deterministic
ordering of partial blocks.
- Cleans up completed blocks from in-flight state to avoid duplicate
partial extraction.

- **Correctness + hygiene alignment**
- Continuation model checks now use `AreModelsCompatible(...)` (instead
of strict string equality).
- Added hostname sanitization in Anthropic error paths (HTTP error
parsing and `httpClient.Do` failures).
- Replaced unconditional Anthropic debug `log.Printf` calls with
`logutil.DevPrintf`.

- **Targeted coverage additions**
  - Added Anthropic tests for:
    - function-call lookup by tool call id
    - tool-use data update + removal
    - `data-tooluse` UI conversion behavior

```go
// usechat-backend.go
func (b *anthropicBackend) RunChatStep(...) (..., []uctypes.GenAIMessage, ...) {
    stopReason, msg, rateLimitInfo, err := anthropic.RunAnthropicChatStep(ctx, sseHandler, chatOpts, cont)
    if msg == nil {
        return stopReason, nil, rateLimitInfo, err
    }
    return stopReason, []uctypes.GenAIMessage{msg}, rateLimitInfo, err
}
```

commit.message:
Add builder-launch strip to AppsFloatingWindow (#2979)

AppsFloatingWindow currently lists local apps but does not provide an
in-context way to open the app builder picker. This change adds a bottom
ghost-style action strip (`+ Build/Edit Apps`) that launches the builder
via Electron preload with `openBuilder(null)`.

- **What changed**
  - Added a new bottom strip action inside `AppsFloatingWindow`:
    - Label: `+ Build/Edit Apps`
- Visual style: ghost-like footer strip with top border, hover state,
full-width click target
- Wired the strip to call the preload API and close the floating window:
    - `getApi().openBuilder(null)` (`null` app id opens the app picker)
- Kept the change scoped to `frontend/app/workspace/widgets.tsx` with no
behavior changes to app-grid item launching.

- **Implementation detail**
  - Imported `getApi` from `@/store/global`
  - Added a memoized handler for builder launch:
  
```tsx
const handleOpenBuilder = useCallback(() => {
    getApi().openBuilder(null);
    onClose();
}, [onClose]);
```

per model — using ANSI box-drawing borders and cursor-up re-render on each

commit.message:
Convert all test files to table-driven format, fix lint, and improve coverage (#173)

* Initial plan

* Convert lambda/handler_test.go to table-driven test format

- Merged 60+ standalone Test functions into grouped table-driven tests
- Each table test has a named 'name' field and t.Parallel() at both
  top-level and inside t.Run closures
- Related tests merged: CreateFunction (7→1), GetFunction (2→1),
  ListFunctions (2→1), DeleteFunction (2→1), UpdateFunctionCode (4→1),
  UpdateFunctionConfiguration (4→1), Invoke (6→1), BackendInvokeFunction
  (4→1), RuntimeServer_Invoke (2→1), RuntimeServer_HTTPEndpoints (3→1),
  RuntimeServer_InvokeStop (2→1), BackendMockDocker (2→1),
  Provider_Init (2→1), FunctionURL_NotFound (3→1), PublishVersion (3→1),
  ListVersionsByFunction (3→1), CreateAlias (6→1), GetAlias (3→1),
  ListAliases (3→1), UpdateAlias (3→1), DeleteAlias (3→1),
  TagsRoute (4→1)
- Direct field assertions used throughout (no generic run() closures)
- Replaced assert.ErrorIs with require.ErrorIs for error assertions
- Fixed nestif complexity using early return pattern
- All helper functions/types preserved unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert cloudformation test files to table-driven format

- Merge all TestParseTemplate_* variants into single TestParseTemplate table test
- Merge TestBackend_CreateStack_*, TestBackend_DescribeStack, TestBackend_UpdateStack,
  TestBackend_DeleteStack, TestBackend_DescribeStackEvents, TestBackend_GetTemplate
  into single table tests per function
- Merge TestBackend_ChangeSet_* into TestBackend_ChangeSet and
  TestBackend_ExecuteChangeSet_* into TestBackend_ExecuteChangeSet
- Merge all TestHandler_* variants per action into single table tests
- Merge TestProvider_Init_WithBackendsProvider and TestProvider_Init_WithConfigProvider
  into single TestProvider_Init_WithConfig table test
- Merge resource creator tests by type: S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, SSM, KMS,
  SecretsManager, UnknownType, and extended types
- All tests use t.Parallel() at top-level and inside each t.Run
- All tests use t.Context(), require/assert from testify
- All tests use named struct fields with direct assertions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert executor_test.go to proper table-driven test format

- Merged 5 TestParse_* functions into single TestParse table
- Merged 2 TestExecutor_PassState_* into TestExecutor_PassState
- Merged TestExecutor_SucceedState + TestExecutor_FailState into TestExecutor_TerminalStates
- Merged 4 wait state tests into TestExecutor_WaitState
- Merged 11 standalone Choice state tests into TestExecutor_ChoiceState
- Converted TestExecutor_ParallelState to single-row table
- Merged 6 Map state tests into TestExecutor_MapState
- Merged 6 path transform tests into TestExecutor_PathTransforms
- Converted TestExecutor_ChainedStates to single-row table
- Merged 9 Task state tests into TestExecutor_TaskState
- Converted TestExecutor_FailError_Method to TestExecutor_FailError table
- Converted TestExecutor_ContextCancellation to single-row table
- Merged Parameters + ResultSelector tests into TestExecutor_Parameters
- Merged 3 Retry tests into TestExecutor_Retry with atomic counters
- Converted TestExecutor_Task_TimeoutSeconds to table with atomic counter
- Converted TestExecutor_UnknownStateType to single-row table
- Use atomic.Int64 for call counters to prevent data races

All tests use t.Parallel() at top-level and inside t.Run.
All assertions use require/assert (no t.Fatal/t.Error).
Uses t.Context() throughout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert stepfunctions/handler_test.go to table-driven test format

- Merge 29 standalone Test functions into 15 table-driven test functions
- Group tests by handler/endpoint; each uses []struct, t.Run, t.Parallel()
- Replace sfnRequest/sfnRequestWithHandler with sfnPost, createSM, startExec helpers
- Add t.Context() via httptest.NewRequestWithContext in all requests
- All lambda setup/check functions include t.Helper()
- context.Context is the first parameter in helpers (revive compliance)
- Struct fields ordered for memory alignment (fieldalignment compliance)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert elasticache/handler_test.go to proper table-driven test format

Merged 28 standalone test functions into 14 table-driven test functions
grouped by handler/operation:
- TestCreateCacheCluster (success, already_exists)
- TestDescribeCacheClusters (describe_specific, describe_all, not_found, not_found_after_delete)
- TestDeleteCacheCluster (success, not_found)
- TestCreateReplicationGroup (success, already_exists)
- TestDescribeReplicationGroups (describe_specific, describe_all, not_found)
- TestDeleteReplicationGroup (success, not_found)
- TestListTagsForResource (cluster_no_tags, replication_group_no_tags, not_found)
- TestBackend (stub_engine_mode, default_engine, list_all)
- TestHandlerMetadata (name, supported_operations, match_priority)
- TestRouteMatcher (non_post, wrong_version, wrong_content_type)
- TestExtractOperation (with_action, empty_action)
- TestExtractResource (cache_cluster_id, replication_group_id)
- TestProvider (init_default, init_with_config)
- TestHandlerUnknownAction (stack_initializes)

Each test uses t.Parallel() at both function and t.Run level, t.Context(),
setup funcs for per-case state, and direct field assertions with
require/assert from testify.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
